title: Research Access — Chart Views Without Active IRB Consent Flag

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
spl:
index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit" Activity="*Research*" OR Context="*Study*" earliest=-30d
| rex field=Context "STUDY:(?<STUDY_ID>[^|]+)"
| lookup irb_active_subjects.csv STUDY_ID PAT_ID OUTPUT consent_status, window_end
| eval in_window=if(_time<strptime(window_end,"%Y-%m-%d"),1,0)
| where consent_status!="Active" OR in_window=0 OR isnull(consent_status)
| stats values(STUDY_ID) as studies dc(PAT_ID) as patients by USER_ID
| sort - patients
value: Correlates research coordinator EHR access with IRB-approved patient rosters to catch out-of-protocol chart reviews.
implementation: (1) Standardize study context logging in Hyperspace build; (2) IRB office owns roster refresh; (3) integrate with Office for Human Research Protections policies.
visualization: Table (user, studies, patients), Bar chart (violations by study).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
